other_observers: none; I later learned that this bird had been seen once or twice before during Spring, 2007.
description: It was a very heavy-bodied hawk, circling lazily well above the tops of the trees.   Its upperparts were solid black, except for a broad white band in the tail.  Its bill was heavy and uniformly light, constrasting strongly with the dark body.  The underparts had a mix of patterns. The wing linings were a solid, dark, blackish color. Most of the rest of the undersurface of the wings was a mottled blackish pattern. Along the leading edge of the wings, most of the way to the outer end, was a small whitish patch on each side.  There was a broad white band in the tail, visible from above and below. Outside this white band was a broader black band, and then a thin terminal white band.Behaviors: The bird was circling lazily well above the tops of tall cottonwood trees for the entire five minutes that I watched it. I presume that it was gliding on thermals.Call: It was silent during the time that I observed it.Plumage: adult
